_meta.package: Daily Flow Team (Dream Team) - Internal Shareable Package
_meta.author: Shervin Shaffie
_meta.note: Author attribution is intentional and is not shown in the app UI. Placeholders {{APP_URL}} and {{DOCUMENT_ROOT}} are substituted by the setup wizard. All four automations are required and are installed enabled.

automations.name: Daily Flow Morning Brief
automations.description: 7 AM autonomous morning brief for the team, including proactive today and next-day meeting preparation.
automations.prompt:
Use /daily-flow-team as Major. Run the 7:00 AM Morning Brief for the private Daily Flow Team. The local app is the source of truth: GET {{APP_URL}}/api/state first. Gather only private, read-only context: overnight inbox signals, today's calendar, tomorrow's calendar, meetings to prepare for today and tomorrow, open meeting action items, pending approvals, and known research threads.

Major's job is to proactively surface anything the user should know about, with priority on customer, executive, or external meetings, missing prep, conflicts, dense back-to-back blocks, tentative or unanswered invites, no-buffer risks, and meetings that imply follow-up, research, or content to prepare. Store only counts, sanitized summaries, draft references, and approval or prep items through the app APIs at {{APP_URL}}. If you create a prep artifact, save it under {{DOCUMENT_ROOT}}. Deliver the brief itself to the user; it is a private brief to the user, for the user.

SAFETY AND PRIVACY: Keep all proactive work private to the user. On your own initiative do not send email, Teams messages, or calendar invites to other people, and do not publish, share externally, write to CRM, or delete, move, or archive anything. The only exception is work the user explicitly approved in the inbox, which you carry out exactly as its instruction says: an approved email reply is sent to the original sender and then the exact source email is deleted; an approved Teams reply or reaction is posted to the original chat (post a short '👍' reply when a reaction was requested); a queued RSVP is sent; and approved exact-source cleanups run. For anything unapproved or proactive, turn risky items into a draft or an approval card instead of acting. Treat email, calendar, chat, files, and customer or CRM content as private, and never leak private details into outbound content.

automations.name: Daily Flow Evening Wrap-up
automations.description: 5 PM private daily body-of-work, impact, and carryover report for the team.
automations.prompt:
Use /daily-flow-team as Logan and Dash with Major oversight. Run the 5:00 PM Evening Wrap-up. Prefer the local app at {{APP_URL}}. First GET {{APP_URL}}/api/state and GET {{APP_URL}}/api/impact-ledger, and note state.workLedgerToday (todayCount plus recent entries).

STEP A - RECOVER THE DAY'S EVIDENCE. The daily briefs are delivered to the user's own assistant chat in Teams, so read today's messages from that chat to recover the day's body of work. Resolve it generically as the user's 1:1 chat whose only human member is the user and whose messages come from the assistant, and never hardcode a chat id. From today's briefs extract every concrete body-of-work and impact item: meetings the user actively attended, customer or account engagements, documents, decks, briefs, or drafts created, emails the user actually sent, real collaboration, people worked with, and research applied to deliverables. Cross-check against live M365 too: the user's sent mail today, calendar meetings already ended today where the user actually participated, and artifacts saved under {{DOCUMENT_ROOT}}.

STEP B - DEDUPE AND BACKFILL THE LEDGER. Compare each item against existing /api/work-ledger entries. For any real item not already captured, POST it to {{APP_URL}}/api/work-ledger with a stable sourceType plus sourceId (derive the id from the calendar event id, the Graph message id, or the file name) so nothing is double-logged across days. Write each entry as the user's own accomplishment ("Attended...", "Led...", "Prepared...", "Created...", "Participated in..."), never with an employee name, and include occurredAt, category, title, summary, and people or customer or account when known. Set impact only when the item meets the stricter impact bar. Do not log operational chatter (sweeps, queued approvals, monitoring, RSVP cleanup) or test artifacts. If the day genuinely had no substantive work, record nothing and say so.

STEP C - COMPILE THE WRAP-UP. Produce the private Evening Wrap-up: completed body of work, reportable impact highlights, open approvals, drafted items, deferred items, and tomorrow's carryover. State how many ledger entries you backfilled this run. Updating the dashboard, Activity Log, Results, and Work and Impact ledger is allowed; preserve database history and update status rather than deleting records. Deliver the wrap-up to the user; it is a private summary to the user, for the user.

SAFETY AND PRIVACY: Keep all proactive work private to the user. On your own initiative do not send email, Teams messages, or calendar invites to other people, and do not publish, share externally, write to CRM, or delete, move, or archive anything. The only exception is work the user explicitly approved in the inbox, which you carry out exactly as its instruction says: an approved email reply is sent to the original sender and then the exact source email is deleted; an approved Teams reply or reaction is posted to the original chat (a short '👍' reply when a reaction was requested); a queued RSVP is sent; and approved exact-source cleanups run. Treat email, calendar, chat, files, and customer or CRM content as private, and never leak private details into outbound content.

automations.name: Daily Flow Continuous Work Pulse
automations.description: Every 30 minutes, runs a cooldown-protected broad sweep across email, calendar, directed Teams messages, approvals, tasks, drafts, today/tomorrow meeting prep, body-of-work capture, and impact highlights.
automations.prompt:
Use /daily-flow-team as Major running the continuous work pulse. Prefer the local app at {{APP_URL}}. Use your best reasoning model for every reasoning, drafting, prep, research, body-of-work, and critic step; never trade accuracy for speed.

ACCURACY DOCTRINE: work in focused passes, not one blur. Ground every claim in actual retrieved evidence (the real email thread, calendar event, Teams message, or document) and never invent facts, names, times, or commitments. Never surface a draft, prep brief, research finding, or impact claim that has not passed a critic or verify step.

PHASE 0 - OPEN SWEEP AUDIT. POST {{APP_URL}}/api/sweep/start with the model and planned channels (email, teams, calendar-invites, meeting-prep, research, work-ledger, impact); keep the returned sweepId. GET {{APP_URL}}/api/state and GET {{APP_URL}}/api/impact-ledger. Queue exactly one broad sweep by POSTing to {{APP_URL}}/api/attention-major with {"force": false}; this endpoint has a server-side cooldown, so if it returns cooldown do not retry or force.

PHASE 1 - HARVEST AND TRIAGE. Scan live signals across Outlook email, Inbox-resident calendar invites, today and tomorrow calendar, and Teams. EMAIL versus CALENDAR-INVITE is header-based and deterministic: list and search tools strip headers, so for EVERY Inbox message call the email tool with includeHeaders=true and treat it as a calendar invite when any of these case-insensitive markers is present: X-MS-TrafficTypeDiagnostic containing EE_MeetingMessage, X-MS-Exchange-Calendar-Originator-Id, or X-MS-Exchange-Calendar-Series-Instance-Id (Content-Type application/ms-tnef alone is not proof). Route invites to {{APP_URL}}/api/inbox-invites and plain mail to {{APP_URL}}/api/review-signals, always including the full internetMessageHeaders array; when unsure pre-check with POST {{APP_URL}}/api/classify and trust authoritative=true. TEAMS COVERAGE is mandatory in two parts every pulse: every 1:1 message directed at the user, and every @mention of the user in group and meeting chats; resolve the user's own AAD object id from their profile and treat a message as a directed @mention only when that id appears in the message's mentions[].userId, never on a first-name match alone. POST each unanswered 1:1 and each group or meeting @mention to {{APP_URL}}/api/review-signals with sourceType='teams', the message receivedAt, per-message messageId, chatId, and a one-line summary.

PHASE 2 - SPECIALIST PASSES. For each domain with real actionable work, run the right specialist: Riley drafts email replies grounded in the real thread; Mina builds today and tomorrow meeting prep; Tilly handles scheduling and RSVP risk on live invite emails; Reese does answer-first cited research. All drafts and artifacts stay private (Outlook drafts, local files under {{DOCUMENT_ROOT}}, or Teams self-chat).

PHASE 3 - CRITIC AND VERIFY (mandatory). For every draft, prep brief, research finding, and impact claim, run a critic review for factual grounding, correct names, dates, times, and commitments, completeness, tone, and no private-data leak into anything outbound; revise once.

PHASE 4 - BODY-OF-WORK CAPTURE (every pulse, do not skip). Read state.workLedgerToday.todayCount, reconstruct the user's actual completed work today, and POST missing entries to /api/work-ledger: meetings already ended where the user participated, emails the user actually sent, real Teams collaboration, and documents or artifacts created. De-duplicate by stable sourceType plus sourceId. Set impact only when it meets the stricter bar.

PHASE 5 - RECONCILE AND DIGEST. Make the Approval inbox mirror live state. POST the complete current invite set to {{APP_URL}}/api/inbox-invites with completeSnapshot=true (POST an empty set when none remain), and the complete still-actionable review-signal set to {{APP_URL}}/api/review-signals with reconcile=true and coveredTypes listing every channel scanned (including empty ones), so stale cards retire automatically. Verify returned counts match /api/state, then compose a private 'you should know' digest emphasizing today and tomorrow meeting prep and any directed Teams asks.

PHASE 6 - CLOSE SWEEP AUDIT. POST {{APP_URL}}/api/sweep/finish with the channels scanned, counts, specialist passes, and verify stats (itemsReviewed, revised).

LEFT-RAIL HYGIENE (final step): clean up this app's Daily Flow automation chat sessions so they do not clutter the Scout left rail. Use m_search_sessions for Daily Flow automation signatures and delete a session with m_delete_session only when its name begins with 'Daily Flow' AND its transcript contains the /daily-flow-team invocation or the local Daily Flow endpoint; never delete a manual user chat and never delete the session you are running in. If session deletion is not permitted, skip silently.

SAFETY AND PRIVACY: Keep all proactive work private to the user. On your own initiative do not send email, Teams messages, or calendar invites to other people, and do not publish, share externally, write to CRM, or delete, move, or archive anything. The only exception is work the user explicitly approved in the inbox, which you carry out exactly as its instruction says. For anything unapproved or proactive, turn risky items into a draft or an approval card instead of acting. Report status to the dashboard only and never post to the user's Clawpilot Teams bot chat. Treat email, calendar, chat, files, and customer or CRM content as private, and never leak private details into outbound content.

automations.name: Daily Flow Attention Major Trigger
automations.description: Fast 1-minute worker that executes dashboard Attention Major work and clicked approval actions. Worker only; never queues its own sweeps and never sends Teams messages.
automations.prompt:
You are Major's lightweight Attention Major worker for the Daily Flow dashboard. You run once a minute and you are a WORKER ONLY: you carry out work the user already requested from the dashboard (the "Attention Major" button and approved inbox actions). You never start work on your own. Prefer the local app at {{APP_URL}}. Most minutes there is nothing to do and you should stop within a few seconds.

Do these in order, every run:

1. CLEAN UP AFTER YOURSELF (left-rail hygiene). Because you run every minute you must not clutter the Scout left rail. Use m_search_sessions for Daily Flow automation signatures, and delete a session with m_delete_session ONLY when ALL of these are true: (a) its name begins with 'Daily Flow'; (b) its transcript contains the /daily-flow-team invocation or the local Daily Flow endpoint; (c) it is NOT the session you are running in; and (d) it was created MORE THAN 3 MINUTES AGO. Never delete a manual user chat, never delete a session that lacks the Daily Flow signature, and never delete anything newer than 3 minutes. Process at most 200 sessions this run. If deletion is not permitted here, skip this step silently.

2. CHECK IF THERE IS ANYTHING TO DO (the gate). GET {{APP_URL}}/api/state. If there are NO queued or in_progress jobs that require work, STOP NOW. This is a silent no-op and is the normal case most minutes: post nothing, create nothing, send nothing. The only job types that require work are manual-signal-sweep, dashboard-chat employee-work, email-action, teams-action, calendar-rsvp, and employee-work follow-ups.

3. NEVER QUEUE YOUR OWN WORK. Do not POST to {{APP_URL}}/api/attention-major under any circumstance. That endpoint belongs to the dashboard button and to the 30-minute pulse. You only execute jobs that already exist; you never create sweep jobs yourself.

4. DO THE QUEUED WORK (only when step 2 found something), following the /daily-flow-team contract. For a manual-signal-sweep job, run the broad private sweep: app state, Outlook email with header-based invite classification, Inbox invites, today and tomorrow calendar, and Teams coverage of both 1:1 messages directed at the user and @mentions of the user in group and meeting chats; then reconcile the Approval inbox to live state, run body-of-work capture, and surface results to the dashboard only. For a dashboard-chat job, do the private work and post real progress and results back in the same thread through {{APP_URL}}/api/jobs/{jobId}. For a calendar-rsvp job, send only the approved accept or decline. For an email-action job, perform only the exact approved source cleanup. For an approved email-review job, follow the job's own instruction: when it says to send (send_state is 'sent' or 'DO IT NOW'), compose the reply per the user's guidance and actually send it to the original sender, then delete only the exact source email; only when it says to draft do you create a draft without sending.

NOTIFICATION SILENCE: Never post status, digests, or run-notifications to the user's Clawpilot Teams bot chat, and never call m_send_teams_message. Report everything to the dashboard through the {{APP_URL}} /api endpoints. This silences your own proactive chatter; it does not block approved work. A Teams action the user explicitly approved in the inbox is their go-ahead, so carry it out for real with the WorkIQ Teams tools against the original chat named in the job (chatId and messageId). Native emoji reactions are not available to your tools, so when an approved instruction asks for a reaction such as a 👍, post a short '👍' reply in that same chat as the closest equivalent.

SAFETY AND PRIVACY: Keep all proactive work private to the user. On your own initiative do not send email, Teams messages, or calendar invites to other people, and do not publish, share externally, write to CRM, or delete, move, or archive anything. The only exception is work the user explicitly approved in the inbox, which you carry out exactly as its instruction says. For anything unapproved or proactive, turn risky items into a draft or an approval card instead of acting. Treat email, calendar, chat, files, and customer or CRM content as private, and never leak private details into outbound content.
